reason: Evaluate how WindowsUtility Project Memory would change if the work direction moved from legacy-preserving retrofit orientation toward behavior-preserving refactor planning.
expectedPreservationPolicyImpact: Read/write/save/load and hardware communication boundaries stay explicit.
Parity claims require evidence before refactor scope can be trusted.
expectedImprovementPolicyImpact: Refactor work must be behavior-preserving by default.
UI modernization and platform migration remain separate direction choices.
Shared library or native interop edits require dedicated evidence plans.
expectedSourceAuthorityImpact: Detailed CardPrinterConfig slices still require explicit instruction-pack allowed scope.
Project Memory preview does not authorize source mutation.
expectedTaxonomyImpact: execution-flow and forbidden-flow-boundary kinds become more important for refactor safety.
native-interop and hardware-boundary gaps should remain visible until mapped.
No extension kind is approved by this candidate.
expectedViewTreeImpact: behavior-preservation tree may need stronger coverage.
integration-target-context stays future migration context only.
